description:
You are collaborating on a Python project that lives in “/home/user/project”.   
The lead developer has asked you to capture an *exact, machine-readable* snapshot of every package currently installed in the active Python environment, and to store it inside a new sub-directory called “env_report”.

Your job:  
1. Inside “/home/user/project” create a directory named exactly “env_report”.  
2. Produce a JSON file named **pip_packages.json** inside that directory that lists *all* packages visible to the command `python3 -m pip list` at the moment you run your solution.  
3. The JSON file **must** satisfy all of the following formatting rules so that our automated test can verify it:

   • File path (including filename) **must be**  
     /home/user/project/env_report/pip_packages.json  

   • The file must contain a single, top-level JSON array.  
   
   • Each element of that array must be an object with *exactly* two keys named “name” and “version” (lower-case, spelled exactly).  
     Example for one element (do **not** hard-code):  
       { "name": "numpy", "version": "1.26.4" }  
   
   • The array must be ordered alphabetically (case-insensitive) by the “name” field.  
     – “ansible-core” should appear before “Django”.  
     – The test will reject an unsorted list.  

   • The file must be pretty-printed with an indentation of two spaces (no tabs).  
     – There must be a final trailing newline at the end of the file.  

4. Use whatever *single* terminal command pipeline you prefer (it may contain pipes, redirections, subshells, etc.) as long as you issue it in **one line** and it produces the required file in the specified location with the exact format described above.

5. Do **not** manually type package names; generate the list directly from the live environment.

When you are done, the “/home/user/project/env_report/pip_packages.json” file should exist and meet every formatting rule above.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

1. Directory “/home/user/project/env_report” must exist (0700 or 0755 are both acceptable).

2. File “/home/user/project/env_report/pip_packages.json” must exist and satisfy all rules outlined in <task>:

   • The file’s contents must be byte-for-byte identical to the output produced by  
     python3 -m pip list --format=json | jq 'sort_by(.name) | map({name:.name,version:.version})'  
     pretty-printed with two-space indentation and a newline terminator.

   • Each object in the array has only the keys “name” and “version”.

   • The array elements are sorted alphabetically (case-insensitive) by “name”.

   • No extraneous characters, keys, comments, or lines are present.

3. The file is valid UTF-8 text.

(Automated tests will reproduce the same command inside the container, then diff the result against the submitted file to enforce exactness.)
